steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank separates two independent clauses. Before the blank: the sculptures … are meant to challenge viewers to consider their own relationships to material wastes (subject: sculptures, verb: are meant). After the blank: her thought provoking works … were no different (subject: works, verb: were). Two independent clauses need a boundary that can join them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — wastes;: A semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice B — wastes and to: Adds and to, turning the second clause into an object phrase — but her works were no different has its own subject and verb, so it cannot follow to. ✗
  • Choice C — wastes,: A comma alone joining two independent clauses is a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice D — wastes: No punctuation between two independent clauses creates a run-on. ✗
That confirms A is correct.
